reviews.text: Quiet clicks and comfortable for spreadsheet work, but mine was unavailable when I tried to reorder.
Good value wireless mouse. The AA battery lasted longer than expected.
Connection is fine once paired, but the stock situation seems inconsistent.
Shape is safe and simple. Not very premium, but easy to recommend if available.
The ergonomic shape helped a lot during long work sessions.
Scroll wheel started skipping after a month, which made it frustrating for documents.
Bluetooth pairing was quick and the USB-C charging is convenient.
Comfort is good, but tracking felt less precise than higher priced mice.
Battery lasts for months and tracking is reliable on a normal desk.
Great fit for medium hands. My wrist felt better than with a flat mouse.
The 2.4GHz receiver has been stable across laptop docks and meeting rooms.
Nothing flashy, but it feels like the safest wireless mouse under this price.
The vertical shape reduced wrist strain after a few days of adjustment.
Tracking is steady and the wireless receiver has not dropped for me.
Takes time to adjust and it is not ideal for fast gaming movements.
Comfortable grip, though the buttons feel lighter than expected.
Silent clicks are genuinely quiet and the connection has been dependable.
Good all-day comfort with no hand fatigue after a week.
Switching between Bluetooth and the receiver is useful, though the button is small.
Precise enough for documents and light design work. Battery life seems strong.
Clicky keys feel fun and responsive for the price.
Several keys started double typing after heavy use.
Good value, but the blue switches are too loud for calls.
RGB looks nice and the full-size layout is convenient.
Red switches are smooth and the case feels sturdy for the price.
Compact layout saves space, but I missed the number pad at first.
Much quieter than blue switch boards and still feels mechanical.
Stable board with no flex while typing or gaming.
Quiet in a shared office and comfortable for everyday typing.
Full-size layout is useful for numbers and shortcuts.
Wireless connection has been stable, with no missed keystrokes so far.
Good for office work, but the typing feel is softer than a mechanical keyboard.
Bright RGB and satisfying keys. The full layout is good for shortcuts.
Feels solid, but the blue switches are loud.
Good keyboard, wrong choice for a quiet office.
The keys are responsive and the stabilizers are acceptable for the price.
Reliable tactile board for work and play, with a familiar full-size layout.
The number pad matters for my work and the brown switches feel balanced.
Comfortable typing feel without the sharp click noise.
Still audible, but much easier to live with than blue switches.
Good bass and comfortable for short listening sessions.
The hinge cracked after a few weeks in my backpack.
Mic works, but voices sound thinner than expected.
Battery life is solid for the price.
Effective noise reduction on buses and clear call quality.
Comfortable for long flights, with no pressure spots after two hours.
The microphone is clear enough for daily meetings.
Sound is balanced rather than bass-heavy, which I prefer.
Mic is clear for meetings and the headset is light.
Comfortable enough for calls, though not as immersive for music.
Wireless connection is stable across a full workday.
On-ear fit gets warm after long sessions.
Neutral sound is useful for editing podcasts and videos.
Very good value if you are fine with a cable.
Comfort is decent and the sound does not exaggerate bass.
Great at a desk, but bulky for travel.
Effective ANC in a compact pair and the carry case is useful.
Good noise control without feeling heavy.
Call quality is better than expected for a compact headset.
Battery lasted several workdays between charges.
Looks good in daylight and setup was simple.
Autofocus hunts constantly, which is distracting on calls.
Fine for occasional calls, but low-light image gets soft.
Good enough for classes if the room is bright.
Reliable autofocus and clear meeting audio.
Good low-light performance compared with cheaper webcams.
Plugged in and worked immediately. Image stays sharp when I move.
The mount is stable and the microphone is usable in a quiet room.
Smooth 60fps video and autofocus keeps up well.
No issues after months, but it needs a separate microphone for best audio.
Image is crisp and motion looks natural.
Better than most 1080p webcams, though still benefits from a desk lamp.
Useful privacy cover and clear picture for normal video calls.
Plug and play setup with no driver issues.
Mic is acceptable and the shutter gives peace of mind.
Image is good in daylight, but grainier at night.
Crisp enough for meetings and easy to mount.
Fixed focus is limiting if you move around.
Good value for basic classes and calls.
Needs decent lighting to look sharp.
Fast for games and noticeably smoother than 60Hz.
Noticeable ghosting in dark scenes.
Text is acceptable, but colors shift from the side.
Good if speed matters more than color accuracy.
Smooth motion and accurate colors for the price.
Great value for a 144Hz IPS screen.
Text is clear and viewing angles are much better than my old VA panel.
Stand is basic, but the panel itself is strong.
Comfortable for spreadsheets and text work all day.
Slim bezels and good color for normal office tasks.
Text clarity is good at 24 inches and 1080p.
Fine for casual games, but not a gaming-focused monitor.
Excellent for photo work with sharp QHD text.
USB-C makes the desk setup tidy.
Color and viewing angles are strong for the price.
Great productivity screen, but the refresh rate is not for competitive gaming.
Crisp QHD gaming with no obvious ghosting.
Strong stand and good color coverage make it useful beyond games.
Text is sharp and 144Hz feels smooth.
Easy to recommend if the budget stretches to it.
The curve feels immersive for games and movies.
Viewing angles are narrow from the side.
Black levels are good, but motion smearing is visible.
Good value if you like curved VA panels.
